cases.blurb: Simple, closed, in-AOI polygon. Passes untouched.
cases.title: Clean sub-4 ha polygon

cases.blurb: First vertex != last. Closing the ring is area-preserving — safe to auto-fix.

findings.human_reason: Exterior ring was not closed (first vertex != last); closing the ring is a safe, area-preserving repair.

cases.title: Unclosed ring

cases.blurb: A stationary GPS logger repeated a vertex. Dropping it leaves the geometry unchanged.

findings.human_reason: Consecutive duplicate vertices detected; they are dropped on cleaning with no change to the geometry.

cases.title: Duplicate vertices

cases.blurb: Spreadsheet wrote [lat, lon]; swapped, it lands outside Vietnam. Never auto-swap.

findings.human_reason: Coordinate order looks swapped ([lat, lon] instead of [lon, lat]); this is the classic spreadsheet-export bug and is not safely auto-correctable -- a human must confirm the order.

cases.title: Lat/lon swap

cases.blurb: EUDR GeoJSON v1.5 does not process interior rings. Dropping the hole changes the boundary.

findings.human_reason: Polygon has interior ring(s) (a hole). The EUDR GeoJson File Description v1.5 rejects interior rings; they cannot be silently dropped because doing so changes the plot boundary. The documented fix is to split the plot into two exterior-only polygons.

cases.title: Doughnut (interior ring)

cases.blurb: Repair fragments one polygon into two. The fix would guess intent — escalate.

findings.human_reason: Invalid geometry; the repair fragmented a polygon into multiple parts, so it cannot be auto-applied without changing the plot's meaning -> human review.

cases.title: Self-intersecting bow-tie

cases.blurb: Art. 9(1)(d): ≥4 ha needs a perimeter polygon. A point cannot prove eligibility.

findings.human_reason: A single point was submitted but the asserted area (5.2 ha) is >= 4.0 ha, which requires a perimeter polygon (EUDR Art. 9(1)(d)). A point cannot prove the <=4 ha point-format eligibility -> human review.

cases.title: Point for a >4 ha plot
